prior.title: Project Drawdown

prior.full_brief:
# Project Drawdown: Drawdown Explorer™ — Research Summary

**Severity: MONITORING**
**Re-check window: Q1 2026** — platform completion and full 140+ solution dataset expected by end of 2026 per stated schedule; revisit when full solution set publishes.

---

## Section 1: What the Research Found

**Headline finding.** On 23 September 2025, Project Drawdown launched the Drawdown Explorer™, described as "the world's most comprehensive climate solutions platform," replacing its prior Solutions Library. The platform provides science-based, sector-spanning analysis of climate solutions categorised across four classifications — Highly Recommended, Worthwhile, Keep Watching, and Not Recommended — across all major emitting sectors.

*Source: "Project Drawdown launches Drawdown Explorer, the world's most comprehensive climate solutions platform," Project Drawdown, 23 September 2025. https://drawdown.org/news/project-drawdown-launches-drawdown-explorer-the-worlds-most-comprehensive-climate-solutions

**Scope and scale.** FACT: "When fully completed in 2026, the Drawdown Explorer will provide detailed, up-to-date information on more than 140 climate solutions that, if scaled, can stop climate change."

*Source: Explorer Launch Announcement, Project Drawdown, 23 September 2025. https://drawdown.org/news/project-drawdown-launches-drawdown-explorer-the-worlds-most-comprehensive-climate-solutions

The platform was last updated 22 September 2025 per the Explorer homepage.

*Source: Drawdown Explorer™, Project Drawdown, last updated 22 September 2025. https://drawdown.org/explorer

**Methodology.** FACT: The assessment draws from "scientific publications, technical reports, white papers, and other public sources from around the world." Assessments are "not based on hidden 'black box' models and instead are based on step-by-step calculations with consistent methods across all solutions." Open-source data are used "as much as possible," and all Highly Recommended solutions carry downloadable spreadsheets with assessment data.

*Source: A Primer on Science-Based Climate Solutions, Project Drawdown, 2024 updated methodology. https://drawdown.org/explorer/primer

**Foundational lineage.** The Drawdown Review, the organisation's second seminal publication, represents "the first major update to our assessment of solutions" and underpins the Explorer's solution database.

*Source: The Drawdown Review, Project Drawdown. https://drawdown.org/publications/the-drawdown-review

**Phase and status.** The platform is live but incomplete. Completion to the full 140+ solution set is scheduled for 2026. This is an in-progress research and intelligence platform, not a finalised research publication. The re-check window for the full dataset is end-2026.

*Source: Explorer Launch Announcement, Project Drawdown, 23 September 2025. https://drawdown.org/news/project-drawdown-launches-drawdown-explorer-the-worlds-most-comprehensive-climate-solutions

**Transportation sector coverage (directly workspace-relevant).** The Explorer's live solution set includes Transportation sector solutions already classified and quantified. From the published Explorer browse view, the following are classified Highly Recommended with quantified potential emissions avoided:

- Mobilize Electric Cars: 1.26–2.30 Gt CO₂-eq/yr
- Increase Carpooling: 0.25–0.96 Gt CO₂-eq/yr
- Mobilize Hybrid Cars: 0.32–0.80 Gt CO₂-eq/yr
- Improve Nonmotorized Transportation: listed Highly Recommended (full range truncated in source)
- Mobilize Electric Bicycles (Private): 0.05–0.11 Gt CO₂-eq/yr

*Source: Drawdown Explorer™ browse view, Project Drawdown, last updated 22 September 2025. https://drawdown.org/explorer

**Sector definition note.** FACT: The Primer states solutions are "classified by where the mitigated greenhouse gas emissions occur." For example, improving lighting efficiency in buildings is counted as an Electricity sector solution because "the emissions it lowers occur at the power plant, not your home." This accounting convention has direct implications for how freight operations attribute and count solution impact.

*Source: A Primer on Science-Based Climate Solutions, Project Drawdown. https://drawdown.org/explorer/primer

**Actor-specific guidance.** FACT: "The open-access online platform offers specific recommendations on how various actors, including policymakers, investors, business leaders, and more, can best unlock and accelerate solutions given their unique resources and capabilities."

*Source: Explorer Launch Announcement, Project Drawdown, 23 September 2025. https://drawdown.org/news/project-drawdown-launches-drawdown-explorer-the-worlds-most-comprehensive-climate-solutions

**Scope of what is and is not a "solution."** FACT: Project Drawdown defines a climate solution as "a physical practice or technology that materially lowers the long-term concentration of greenhouse gases (and other warming pollution, such as black carbon) in the atmosphere." Policies, regulations, laws, investments, grants, campaigns, social movements, and protests are explicitly defined as "levers," not solutions.

*Source: A Primer on Science-Based Climate Solutions, Project Drawdown. https://drawdown.org/explorer/primer

**Methodological limitation.** The Drawdown Review notes that a June 2022 update added 11 new solutions, with the instruction that "for the latest data and conclusions, please refer to our online solutions summaries." The Drawdown Explorer is the current authoritative dataset; the Drawdown Review publication predates the 2024 methodology update.

*Source: The Drawdown Review, Project Drawdown. https://drawdown.org/publications/the-drawdown-review

---

## Section 2: Why This Finding Matters Operationally and Commercially

**For workspaces operating across air, road, and ocean freight in a forwarding role, Project Drawdown's sector taxonomy and solution classification carry three direct operational and commercial implications.**

**Implication 1 — client sustainability briefs and ESG reporting.**
Operational implication: Clients operating under CSRD, ISSB S2, or voluntary science-based targets are increasingly expected to demonstrate that their supply chain partners are engaging with evidence-based decarbonisation pathways. The Drawdown Explorer, now the most publicly cited open-access science-based solutions database, is increasingly the reference frame against which client sustainability teams will evaluate supplier claims. A workspace that cannot map its own initiatives to Drawdown's classified solutions (Highly Recommended vs. Worthwhile vs. Keep Watching) risks being filtered out of sustainability-aligned procurement decisions.

**Implication 2 — transportation solution gap.**
Analytical inference: The Explorer's current transportation solutions are predominantly passenger-mode (electric cars, carpooling, e-bikes) and light-road. No dedicated freight-specific or air-cargo-specific solution classification is visible in the currently published browse view. This is consistent with Drawdown's historical coverage emphasis and creates a credibility gap: the most cited open-access database does not yet specifically classify SAF for air freight or alternative maritime fuels for ocean freight as standalone named solutions with quantified potential ranges. Workspaces using Drawdown as a reference in client materials should note this gap explicitly rather than claiming Drawdown endorsement for SAF or ocean decarbonisation at scale.

**Implication 3 — "speed of action" classification.**
Operational implication: The Explorer introduces a "Speed of Action" dimension — including an "Emergency Brake" category for solutions that can achieve rapid near-term emission reductions. For workspaces with clients under near-term Science Based Targets, the Emergency Brake designation signals which solutions can be credibly cited for short-horizon commitments versus which are long-cycle investments. From the published data, solutions rated Emergency Brake include Deploy Alternative Refrigerants (2.50–2.70 Gt CO₂-eq/yr), Deploy Clean Cooking, Increase Centralized Composting, and Improve Landfill Management variants — none directly in freight transport modes. Gradual-rated solutions cover the transport sector entries.

*Source: Drawdown Explorer™ browse view, Project Drawdown, last updated 22 September 2025. https://drawdown.org/explorer

---

## Section 3: What the Finding Changes for Strategy, Claims, or Decisions

**Decision 1 — Sustainability claims audit.** Workspaces currently citing Project Drawdown in client proposals, RFP responses, or ESG reports should audit those citations against the live Explorer classification. If a cited solution is classified "Worthwhile" or "Keep Watching" rather than "Highly Recommended," that classification difference is material. Analytical inference: a client sustainability team reviewing a forwarding partner's decarbonisation proposal will increasingly check the Explorer classification; an unchecked citation of a lower-classification solution as a primary commitment undermines the workspace's credibility.

**Decision 2 — SAF and maritime fuel claims require separate grounding.** Analytical inference: Because the Explorer does not yet visibly classify SAF-for-aviation-freight or green ammonia/methanol for ocean freight as discrete named solutions, workspaces should not cite Drawdown as the authority for SAF or maritime fuel decarbonisation claims. Those claims require grounding in IMO, ICAO, or the ICCT corpus, not Drawdown. Overreliance on Drawdown for fuel-switch claims exposes the workspace to client-side challenge.

**Decision 3 — Drawdown Capital Coalition.** The Explorer launch page references a "Drawdown Capital Coalition" described as "Maximizing the impact of climate investments." Analytical inference: for workspaces engaging clients on green freight financing or sustainability-linked lending, the Drawdown Capital Coalition is a potential institutional context worth monitoring as a channel for third-party validation of investment decisions. Research gap: the specific scope, membership, and access conditions of the Drawdown Capital Coalition are not detailed in the source blocks provided; further retrieval is needed before any claim about participation or access can be made.

**Decision 4 — geographic intelligence layer.** FACT: The Explorer includes "interactive maps showing where action can have the greatest impact." For workspaces with multi-regional trade lanes (Americas, Europe, Asia), this geographic layer, once fully populated at the 140+ solution level, will allow region-specific solution matching against client trade corridors. Analytical inference: this is a forward capability — the full geographic dataset is not yet published — but workspaces should monitor it as a tool for differentiating sustainability advisory to clients by trade lane.

*Source: Drawdown Explorer™, Project Drawdown, last updated 22 September 2025. https://drawdown.org/explorer

---

## Section 4: Client Conversation Talking Points and Public Position

**What the workspace can credibly say:**

- "Project Drawdown's Drawdown Explorer, launched September 2025, is the leading open-access, peer-reviewed database of science-based climate solutions. We track its classifications to evaluate which decarbonisation initiatives in our operations are Highly Recommended versus emerging or unproven."
- "Drawdown's taxonomy distinguishes Emergency Brake solutions — those able to cut emissions rapidly — from Gradual solutions. In our core transport modes, the primary decarbonisation levers are rated Gradual, which is consistent with multi-year fleet and fuel transition timelines."
- "The platform explicitly defines policies and financial instruments as 'levers' rather than solutions. We use this framing to separate what we are investing in physically from the regulatory and financial mechanisms that support it — which is the accurate way to present a science-based decarbonisation strategy."

**Questions the workspace can pose to clients:**
- "Which Project Drawdown solution classifications does your own sustainability strategy currently reference, and have you reviewed whether those classifications have been updated in the 2024 methodology refresh?"
- "For your near-term SBTi milestones, are you focused on Emergency Brake solutions, or are your key initiatives in the Gradual category? Understanding this helps us align modal and routing decisions to your actual commitment horizon."

**Pitfalls to avoid:**

- Do not cite Drawdown as validating SAF or maritime green fuel pathways specifically — the Explorer's current published transportation solutions do not yet include those as named classifications with quantified ranges. Legal Confirmation Required: whether citing Drawdown in a contractual sustainability clause as authority for specific fuel-switch obligations would be supported by the Explorer's current published content requires counsel review.
- Do not cite the 2020 Drawdown Review publication figures as current — Project Drawdown explicitly states the Review's data has been superseded and directs readers to the online Explorer for current numbers.
- Do not claim Drawdown classification as a compliance standard — it is a science-based advisory database, not a regulatory instrument.

*Source: A Primer on Science-Based Climate Solutions, Project Drawdown. https://drawdown.org/explorer/primer; The Drawdown Review, Project Drawdown. https://drawdown.org/publications/the-drawdown-review

---

## Section 5: What the Finding Does Not Resolve

**Open question 1 — Freight-specific solution coverage.** The Explorer in its current (pre-full-completion) state does not appear to include dedicated classifications for air freight decarbonisation (SAF mandates, aircraft efficiency), ocean freight (alternative fuels, CII ratings, green corridors), or road freight at scale. Whether these will appear as the platform completes to 140+ solutions in 2026 is stated as pending but not detailed. *Specific solution taxonomy for freight modes not available from primary sources as of 2026-05-27.* This is the most material open question for workspace use.

**Open question 2 — Drawdown Capital Coalition access and scope.** The Coalition is referenced but not described in the source blocks. No membership criteria, investment thresholds, or freight-sector participation details are available. *Specific participation criteria and freight-sector scope not available from primary sources as of 2026-05-27.*

**Open question 3 — Regulatory cross-reference.** Drawdown is a scientific advisory body, not a regulatory authority. The Explorer's classifications do not map directly to EU ETS, CSRD disclosure requirements, IMO GHG Strategy, or CORSIA eligibility criteria. The relationship between Drawdown's "Highly Recommended" status and regulatory compliance eligibility under any specific jurisdiction is unresolved and requires separate regulatory tracking. No authoritative guidance on this mapping has been published as of 2026-05-27.

**Converging and diverging research.** The ICCT, IMO MEPC, and ICAO produce transport-sector-specific decarbonisation analysis that partially overlaps with Drawdown's Transportation sector but uses different methodological conventions and policy-compliance framing. These are complementary, not interchangeable, and should not be conflated in client-facing materials.

---

## New Sources Identified

| Source Name | URL | Tier estimate (1–7) | Why this source matters |
|---|---|---|---|
| Drawdown Explorer™ – Official Launch Announcement | https://drawdown.org/news/project-drawdown-launches-drawdown-explorer-the-worlds-most-comprehensive-climate-solutions | 2 | Official Project Drawdown press release providing launch date, platform scope, 140+ solution count, 2026 completion schedule, and executive/external endorsement quotes — primary authority for Explorer facts. |
| A Primer on Science-Based Climate Solutions | https://drawdown.org/explorer/primer | 2 | Authoritative official methodology primer detailing solution taxonomy, sector classification conventions, open-source data use, and actor-specific guidance — essential for claims about how Drawdown classifies and evaluates solutions. |
| The Drawdown Review | https://drawdown.org/publications/the-drawdown-review | 2 | Project Drawdown's second seminal publication; foundational peer-reviewed update that underpins the Explorer's solution database; also source of explicit note that Review figures are superseded by the online Explorer. |

---
